config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi dasar yang dilindungi, karena tanpa itu masyarakat mudah berubah menjadi alat untuk menekan orang yang lemah atau minoritas. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh dipakai sebagai alasan untuk mengorbankan kebebasan, martabat, dan keadilan bagi individu. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dihormati dan kepentingan bersama dicapai tanpa menindas orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the individual; in the United States, I see individual rights as the default starting point, not something society can override whenever it is convenient. That said, rights are not absolute in every case, because public safety, public health, and preventing real harm can justify limits, but those limits need to be narrow and accountable. I am wary of the phrase "the interests of society" because it can be used to silence minorities or excuse abuses in the name of the majority. A healthy society protects the common good by protecting individual liberty first, not by treating the person as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diutamakan di atas hak individu secara umum. Di Indonesia, saya melihat terlalu sering alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan pembungkaman, persekusi minoritas, atau keputusan sepihak yang merugikan warga biasa, jadi saya sangat curiga pada frasa itu. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, terutama untuk keselamatan publik dan ketertiban, tetapi batasnya harus jelas, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau hak individu tidak jadi dasar, masyarakat justru mudah berubah menjadi alat mayoritas untuk menekan yang lemah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should generally take priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, that idea is too easy to abuse, because "society" often becomes a slogan for the majority, the government, or powerful groups to justify silencing people they do not like. I do accept that individual rights can be limited for real public safety and public health reasons, but those limits have to be narrow, evidence-based, and accountable, not a blank check. In my view, a free society is strongest when it treats individual rights as the foundation and only restricts them when there is a clear and serious necessity.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju dengan pernyataan itu, karena di Indonesia saya melihat alasan “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan minoritas dan membenarkan tindakan sewenang-wenang. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru harus dicapai dengan melindungi hak individu sebagai batas utama, bukan dengan menempatkannya di bawah kehendak mayoritas atau negara. Kalau ada pembatasan demi keselamatan publik atau ketertiban, itu harus sangat jelas, proporsional, dan bisa diuji, bukan slogan kosong yang mudah disalahgunakan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu menjaga kepentingan umum tanpa menginjak martabat orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over individual rights in general. From an American point of view, that framing is dangerous because it can turn into a blank check for the majority, the state, or powerful institutions to override unpopular people and call it the common good. I do accept limited restrictions when there is a real, specific public safety or public health need, but those limits should be narrow, evidence-based, and easy to challenge. A society that treats individual rights as disposable is not protecting the common good; it is just making it easier to abuse people.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
